document.docAuthor: Brescovit, Antonio Domingos, Zampaulo, Robson De Almeida, Cizauskas, Igor & Pedroso, Leandro
document.docTitle: Speocera piquira Brescovit & Zampaulo & Cizauskas & Pedroso 2022, sp. nov.
document.masterDocTitle: Three new subterranean species of the genus Speocera (Araneae, Ochyroceratidae) from caves of the Carajás area in the state of Pará, Brazil

mods:titleInfo.mods:title: Three new subterranean species of the genus Speocera (Araneae, Ochyroceratidae) from caves of the Carajás area in the state of Pará, Brazil

mods:name.mods:affiliation: Laboratório de Coleções Zoológicas, Instituto Butantan, Av. Vital Brasil, 1500, Butantã, São Paulo-São Paulo, Brazil, 05503 - 900 Gerencia de Licenciamento Ambiental e Espeleologia, Vale SA, Avenida Doutor Marco Paulo Simon Jardim, 34006 - 200 Nova Lima- Minas Gerais, Brazil
Organização de Apoio à Pesquisa da Biodiversidade (OAPBio), Rua Frei Inácio da Conceição 238, 05362 - 040, São Paulo, São Paulo, Brazil

Laboratório de Coleções Zoológicas, Instituto Butantan, Av. Vital Brasil, 1500, Butantã, São Paulo-São Paulo, Brazil, 05503 - 900 Gerencia de Licenciamento Ambiental e Espeleologia, Vale SA, Avenida Doutor Marco Paulo Simon Jardim, 34006 - 200 Nova Lima- Minas Gerais, Brazil

subSubSection.paragraph: Types . Male holotype from Cave SFX_0015 ( 6°25′54,49″S 51°50′49,88″W ), São Félix do Xingu, Pará, Brazil, 23/II/2018 , Ativo Ambiental col., deposited in IBSP 264231. Paratypes : female from Cave SFX_0024 ( 6°23′28″S 51°52′28″W ), São Félix do Xingu, Pará, Brazil, 01/II/2018 , Ativo Ambiental col. (IBSP 264256); two males and two females from Cave SFX_0057 ( 6°24′06″S 51°54′08″W ), same locality, 01/II/2018 , Ativo Ambiental col. (ex IBSP 264258, ISLA ); one male and two females from Cave SFX_0025 ( 6°23′27″S 51°52′30″W ), same locality, 18/VII/2018 , Ativo Ambiental col. (MPEG 37253, ex IBSP 264236).

caption.paragraph:
FIGURE 12.
Speocera piquira
sp. nov. , m
ale (IBSP 97742). A Habitus, dorsal view. B–D Left palp. B Retrolateral view C Ventral view D Prolateral view E Embolus, ventral view F Retrolateral view. Abbreviations: B, bulb; C, conductor; SC, associated sclerites (SCI, SCII, SCIII and SCIV); SO, spermatic opening.

FIGURE 13.
Speocera piquira
sp. nov.
, male (IBSP 97735), SEM images. A chelicerae, frontal view B epiandrous area, abdomen, ventral view C colulus, abdomen, ventral view D cymbium, tarsal organ, detail, distal area E male palp, ventral view F same, embolus, retrolateral view. Abbreviations: B, bulb; C, conductor; SC, associated sclerites (SCI).

FIGURE 14.
Speocera piquira
sp. nov. A–B
Male palp, IBSP 97735, SEM images. A embolus, ventral view B same, prolateral view C–F female, IBSP 97750. C ocular area, frontal view D chelicerae, frontal view E pedipalp, distal area F tarsal organ, detail, distal area. Abbreviations: B, bulb; C, conductor; SC, associated sclerites (SCI, SCII, SCIII and SCIV); SO, spermatic opening.

subSubSection.paragraph: Etymology . The specific name is derived from the Tupi language and means small, as is the case with spiders of this genus. Noun in apposition.

Diagnosis . Males of
Speocera piquira
sp. nov.
differs by the male having very long and curved distally SCI in the embolus (
Figs 12E – F
;
14A – B
)—while in
S. pinima
sp. nov.
it is pentagonal and the small conductor, fig. 1E, and presenting long SCI and conductor with large base in in
S. babau
sp. nov.
, fig. 7E. The female of
Speocera piquira
sp. nov.
differ from
S. pinima
sp. nov.
and
S. babau
sp. nov.
by its shorter and almost straight afferent spermathecae and S-shaped lateral plates (
Figs. 15D – F
) in the genitalia.

caption.paragraph:
FIGURE 15.
Speocera piquira
sp. nov .
, female (IBSP 97744). A habitus, dorsal view. B abdomen, lateral view C Same, ventral view. D genitalia, lateral view. E same, enzyme cleared, dorsal view (IBSP 265250) F Same, dorsal view. Abbreviations: AS, afferent spermathecae; CC, copulatory channel; LP, lateral plate; S, spermathecae; TD, terminal depression; VP, ventral plate.

FIGURE 16.
Speocera piquira
sp. nov .
, female (IBSP 97750), SEM images. A pedipalp, tibia, trichobotria, dorsal view B colulus, abdomen, ventral view C genitalia, ventral plate, D same, lateral plate. Abbreviations: CC, copulatory channel; LP, lateral plate; S, spermathecae; TD, terminal depression; VP, ventral plate.

subSubSection.paragraph: Variation . 10 ♂♂ : total length 1.2 – 1.3; carapace 0.45 – 0.6; femur I 0.45 – 0.67; 10 ♀♀ : total length 0.95 – 1.15; carapace 0.5 – 0.7; femur I 0.43 – 063.

Distribution . Widely distributed in iron caves of the Carajás ferruginous outcroops, municipalities of Canaã dos Carajás, Curionópolis, Parauapebas and São Félix do Xingu, state of Pará , Brazil in a range of approximately 250 km ( Fig. 19B, C ).
